sub_mechanisms: M_1: assemble a fixed open-ended prompt corpus that serves as the test category
M_2: collect both models' responses (or response distributions) to every prompt
M_3: check natural-transformation property across structured prompt-prompt relationships (e.g., paraphrase, translation)
M_4: emit representability verdict: models are equivalent iff their natural transformations on the prompt corpus agree
stripped_llm_application: A model-equivalence verifier that treats each model's prompt-response function as a functor on a fixed prompt corpus and tests whether two models induce the same natural transformation; equivalence holds iff transformations agree on every structurally-related prompt pair.
novelty_claim_stripped: Standard model-equivalence checks use top-1 answer agreement on benchmarks. The candidate adds a category-theoretic natural-transformation check across structured prompt-prompt relationships (paraphrase consistency, translation consistency) as a stronger equivalence criterion.
